result.stdout:
USAGE:
    ByteHide.dll [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help       Prints help information   
    -v, --version    Prints version information
                                               
COMMANDS:
    shield    All the functions that Bytehide shield offers

OPTIONS:

result.stdout:
USAGE:
    ByteHide.dll shield [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information
                                         
COMMANDS:
    protect <APPLICATION PATH> <OUTPUT DIRECTORY PATH>    Protect your          
                                                          application with a    
                                                          single execution,     
                                                          ideal for configuring 
                                                          your scripts and      
                                                          automating msbuild    
    check <APPLICATION PATH>                              Check if the          
                                                          application is        
                                                          protected             
    protect-asp-javascript <PROJECT PATH>                 Apply obfuscation to  
                                                          JavaScript files in   
                                                          your ASP.NET          
                                                          application. This     
                                                          command allows you to 
                                                          secure both           
                                                          user-defined and core 
                                                          ASP.NET scripts in a  
                                                          single execution,     
                                                          making it ideal for   
                                                          automated protection  
                                                          in your build process 
    protect-javascript <FOLDER PATH>                      Apply obfuscation to  
                                                          JavaScript files. This
                                                          command allows you to 
                                                          secure .js scripts in 
                                                          a single execution,   
                                                          making it ideal for   
                                                          automated protection  
                                                          in your build process

ARGUMENTS:
    <APPLICATION PATH>    Path of the application
                                                 
OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

commands.payload:

    ByteHide.dll shield check <APPLICATION PATH> [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    <APPLICATION PATH>    Path of the application
                                                 
OPTIONS:
    -h, --help    Prints help information

result.stdout:
USAGE:
    ByteHide.dll shield protect <APPLICATION PATH> <OUTPUT DIRECTORY PATH> [
OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    <APPLICATION PATH>         Path of the application                 
    <OUTPUT DIRECTORY PATH>    Path of the output application protected
                                                                       
OPTIONS:
    -h, --help                 Prints help information                          
        --pdb-path             Path of the debug symbols (.pdb) path. If not    
                               provided, Shield will not generate a new .pdb    
                               file                                             
        --preset               Selects the type of preset maximum, balance or   
                               optimized that should be applied                 
    -c, --configuration        Path of the application shield configuration. If 
                               not provided, the default configuration will be  
                               used                                             
        --sign-key             Path of the sign key to sign the application. If 
                               not provided, the application will not be signed 
        --sign-key-password    Password of the sign key to sign the application.
                               If not provided, the application will not be     
                               signed                                           
    -k, --project-token        The project token to link this protection.       
                               Ignored if configuration file provided, required 
                               if configuration is not present                  
    -p, --protections          The protection IDs split by ',' (comma). I.e:    
                               rename,anti_debug

commands.payload:
USAGE:
    ByteHide.dll shield protect <APPLICATION PATH> <OUTPUT DIRECTORY PATH> [
OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    <APPLICATION PATH>         Path of the application                 
    <OUTPUT DIRECTORY PATH>    Path of the output application protected
                                                                       
OPTIONS:
    -h, --help                 Prints help information                          
        --pdb-path             Path of the debug symbols (.pdb) path. If not    
                               provided, Shield will not generate a new .pdb    
                               file                                             
        --preset               Selects the type of preset maximum, balance or   
                               optimized that should be applied                 
    -c, --configuration        Path of the application shield configuration. If 
                               not provided, the default configuration will be  
                               used                                             
        --sign-key             Path of the sign key to sign the application. If 
                               not provided, the application will not be signed 
        --sign-key-password    Password of the sign key to sign the application.
                               If not provided, the application will not be     
                               signed                                           
    -k, --project-token        The project token to link this protection.       
                               Ignored if configuration file provided, required 
                               if configuration is not present                  
    -p, --protections          The protection IDs split by ',' (comma). I.e:    
                               rename,anti_debug

result.stdout:
USAGE:
    ByteHide.dll shield protect-asp-javascript <PROJECT PATH> [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    <PROJECT PATH>    Specifies the path of the ASP.NET application to be       
                      protected                                                 
                                                                                
OPTIONS:
    -h, --help                       Prints help information                    
        --replace-project            If set to true, the original project files 
                                     will be replaced with the obfuscated       
                                     versions                                   
        --obfuscate-aspcore          Indicates whether to obfuscate core ASP.NET
                                     scripts (e.g., webforms.js) in addition to 
                                     user-defined scripts                       
        --output-directory-prefix    Specifies a prefix for the output directory
                                     name where the obfuscated project will be  
                                     saved                                      
        --output-directory           Specifies the path of the output directory 
                                     where the obfuscated project will be saved.
                                     If not provided, a default directory will  
                                     be used

commands.payload:
USAGE:
    ByteHide.dll shield protect-asp-javascript <PROJECT PATH> [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    <PROJECT PATH>    Specifies the path of the ASP.NET application to be       
                      protected                                                 
                                                                                
OPTIONS:
    -h, --help                       Prints help information                    
        --replace-project            If set to true, the original project files 
                                     will be replaced with the obfuscated       
                                     versions                                   
        --obfuscate-aspcore          Indicates whether to obfuscate core ASP.NET
                                     scripts (e.g., webforms.js) in addition to 
                                     user-defined scripts                       
        --output-directory-prefix    Specifies a prefix for the output directory
                                     name where the obfuscated project will be  
                                     saved                                      
        --output-directory           Specifies the path of the output directory 
                                     where the obfuscated project will be saved.
                                     If not provided, a default directory will

result.stdout:
USAGE:
    ByteHide.dll shield protect-javascript <FOLDER PATH> [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    <FOLDER PATH>    Specifies the path of the folder that contains .js files to
                     be protected                                               
                                                                                
OPTIONS:
    -h, --help                       Prints help information                    
        --script-match               Specifies the regex expression to match the
                                     file names of the directory that must be   
                                     obfuscated                                 
        --replace-project            If set to true, the original project files 
                                     will be replaced with the obfuscated       
                                     versions                                   
        --output-directory-prefix    Specifies a prefix for the output directory
                                     name where the obfuscated project will be  
                                     saved                                      
        --output-directory           Specifies the path of the output directory 
                                     where the obfuscated project will be saved.
                                     If not provided, a default directory will  
                                     be used

commands.payload:
USAGE:
    ByteHide.dll shield protect-javascript <FOLDER PATH> [OPTIONS]

ARGUMENTS:
    <FOLDER PATH>    Specifies the path of the folder that contains .js files to
                     be protected                                               
                                                                                
OPTIONS:

        --script-match               Specifies the regex expression to match the
                                     file names of the directory that must be   
                                     obfuscated                                 
        --replace-project            If set to true, the original project files 
                                     will be replaced with the obfuscated       
                                     versions                                   
        --output-directory-prefix    Specifies a prefix for the output directory

                                     saved                                      
        --output-directory           Specifies the path of the output directory 
                                     where the obfuscated project will be saved.
                                     If not provided, a default directory will  
                                     be used
